sections.paragraph:
m. מְלֵאָה f. verbal adj.-

(1) filling, with an acc. of place, Isa 6:1, שׁוּלָיו מְלֵאִים אֶת־הַהֵיכָל “his train (was) filling (filled) the temple.” Jer 23:24. Compare the root, Kal No. 1, a.

(2) intransitive, filled, full, as כֶּסֶף מָלֵא full money, i.e. the just price, Gen 23:9 followed by an accus. Deu 6:11, בָּתִּים מְלֵאִים כָּל־טוּב “houses full of all good things.” Deu 34:9 Isa 51:20 Amo 2:13 and a genit. Jer 6:11, מְלֵא יָמִים full of days, advanced in age. Isa 1:21. Once with a pleonastic dat. מְלֵאָה לָהּ Amo 2:13.-A full wind is a strong, violent wind. Jer 4:12, רוּחַ מָלֵא מֵאֵלֶּה “a wind stronger than (is needful for) these” (i.e. than for winnowing); (comp. Jer 4:5 12:6 ).

Neutr. מָלֵא-(a) subst. fulness. Psal. 73:10 , מֵי מָלֵא waters of fulness, i.e. full, abundant.-(b) adv. fully, i.e. with a full voice, Jer 12:6 in full number, Nah 1:10.
